notes:
Smooth game play running Mordhau Alpha #20,  between 70-100fps with nvidia 415.27 and Proton 3.16-7

Detailed information on:

https://mordhau.com/forum/topic/13958/howto-play-mordhau-alpha-on-linux-steam-play/
title: MORDHAU

notes: Everything works.
title: MORDHAU

notes: Using RX480 it plays on high at 60fps consistently. There are some frame drops, but it runs smoothly! I have only one system crash, I don't know its cause!
title: MORDHAU

notes: Had to turn graphics down a bit, but it works.

notes: Works like intended.

notes: Worked out of the box, noticed no issues or bugs. Played the tutorial and multiplayer.

notes: Tried Proton 4.2-3, but it's the same story as the 3.16-9 : the game start, take like 45 sec to load, then crash, staying in the running state in Steam.

notes: I get about 70fps on the settings the game chose by default for my GTX 1080.  No obvious issues in multiplayer.  Did crash once during loading but I don't know if that's wine's fault or the game itself.

notes: Works great out of the box; multiplayer is a little slow to put together a battle royale on the west coast, but the game works fine.

notes: runs 80-100 fps with a gtx 1080 at 1440p at ultra to high settings, no tweaks required
title: MORDHAU

notes: No issues at all. It took a while the first time I started the game to get in to the game but after that it launches and plays without any issues.
title: MORDHAU

notes: No tweaks.  Runs perfectly well right from clicking "install" and then "play" in Steam; 1080p @ 60fps on Ultra settings across the board just fine.  To hit steady 75fps to match my Monitor's refresh of 75Hz, I had to turn most graphics settings to from Ultra to High, but it holds steady in the  mid 70fps range on my system.
title: MORDHAU

notes: Everything's great.

notes: Worked out of the box, maintains stable 90 fps with all settings on ultra
title: MORDHAU

notes: Runs great without the need of any tweaks (in my case). Runs around 70-100 FPS in Team Deathmatch games and around 65-90 in Frontline. All settings maxed except Screen Space Reflections, which is on high. I'd say the FPS is around that as on Windows but haven't benchmarked thoroughly. Haven't tried other modes. Proton: 4.2-3

notes: Runs smooth with 55-144fps, no bugs detected, guess performance will be patched later. Tested with Proton 4.2-3

notes: No idea how it runs on Windows but it's smooth as butter on Linux for me. Runs amazing.

notes: Runs well out of the box but crashes every 2-3 hours. Very annoying when in game...
title: MORDHAU

notes: Runs perfectly fine using nvidia-dkms 99.9% of the time. The main issues I encountered had to do with exceeding 60fps (caused something akin to like input lag mixed with no input. like dragging mouse would randomly stop mouse input when above 60fps. The higher your frames, the more extreme the effect). Changing pretty much any video settings requires a restart. Sometimes on map load, you will randomly get the input lag you would get above 60fps and then have to rejoin the server to solve the problem.
title: MORDHAU

notes: Works out of the box.

notes: Worked perfectly fine now out of the box on nvidia driver 430.14, when I was on 430.09 before it tended to stutter more. I have not experienced any crashed whatsoever (yet)

notes:
The first time I ran the tutorial I was unable to select anything or change my view, I suspect however that it was a bug within the game and not related to Linux compatibility. 

The game runs smooth at 60fps and I have not found a single issue. Pretty much plug and play.

notes:
Works mostly fine out of the box. Load times are a bit wacky when launching the game or a map for the first time. 
A few issues I've noticed: 
-keyboard occasionally stops being able to type. Tabbing in and out of game seems to fix this.
-randomly, game will steadily chug down to under 20fps and require a restart. Unsure why.
-Some weird input lag at 60fps+ that requires a restart

notes: Runs with way less fps compared to my windows machine. Had to go from High settings to Normal on nearly all settings and still get less fps. But more worse are the constant freezes / massive drops in frametimes in between, which makes combat really difficult
title: MORDHAU

notes: Works and performs great out of the box. I was initially running it on my mint machine, and even though I had decent performance there, it was frequently freezing my pc entirelly, so I had to reboot to get it to work again. Thus far this never happened on Manjaro, so I'm very happy about it.

notes: Works out of the box with a 1-5% decrease in performance as compared to Windows 10.
title: MORDHAU

notes: 64 players games can result in frame drops in map centers, game related not Proton related.

notes: Works great out of the box. No tweaks were needed. Mordhau uses VAC so no need to worry about anti-cheat.

notes: Works perfectly on proton 4.2-5!!

notes: Game runs great, even in 4K but I had to lower settings a bit (reduce shader quality) to get a smooth framerate. It has crashed on me once when loading a new map but that might have happened on Windows too. All in all, works great!
title: MORDHAU

notes: Game runs fine out-of-box(4.2.5 rather than 4.2.4 however, protondb devs pls update). Definitely some frame drops compared to Windows, and it hitches often enough I wouldn't dare take it into Frontline or BR. Still, solid enough for dueling.
title: MORDHAU

notes: Worked perfectly until very recently; not sure if caused by some Linux or Mordhau update, but for some days I get occasional hitches where the frame freezes for 1 to 3 seconds no matter the graphic settings, especially amid fierce combat. Needless to say that this is a deal breaker for a game like Mordhau. When frames resume I'm usually dead.
title: MORDHAU

notes: No issues!

notes: Game ran perfectly for nearly a month, but something changed a few days ago because there is horrible stuttering where I used to get smooth fps. I suspect it is an issue with Proton 4.2.5

notes: Just installed and played. Haven't had any issues, solid 60fps on almost all max graphics.

notes: No problems here, ran amazing for me

notes: Everything works amazingly, Seems like it black screens on launch but is fixed by just clicking
title: MORDHAU

notes:
Works out of the box perfectly. About 45-60 fps with everything maxed out in 1440p, so it really runs great. With Feral's Gamemode I get about ~7 fps more, so around 50-65. Framerate is very stable.

There's a little bit of stutters the first minute after entering a server/a new map, but after that it's flawless. Controller fine too without having to rebind anything, though this game might be a bit hard to play with a controller.
title: MORDHAU

notes: Only issue was a bit screen-tearing, which however was only noticeable quite rarely
title: MORDHAU

notes: The only issue is alt+tab which does not hide game window
title: MORDHAU

notes: This game runs perfectly, but only with Proton 3.16-9. With the earlier version, 3.16-4, there's audio issues and you can hear every sound on the map playing at the same time. If you go up a version, to 4.2-5, there's issues with random freezing. Every few minutes the game will freeze for a second or so, which means guaranteed death if you're fighting someone. This makes the game extremely frustrating to play --- if you're getting similar issues I suggest trying Proton 3.16-9.
title: MORDHAU

notes: Game runs great, good performance after the first game (typical even on Windows, I'm told), but Alt-Tab does not hide the game window

title: MORDHAU

notes: The game runs perfectly out of the box and I am getting 75+ fps on high settings. However using Proton version 4.2-6 makes the game to lag in certain situations (where there are loads of enemies nearby) which makes it less than ideal. I'll stick with 3.16-9 for now. One more thing, Alt-tab turns the game into a plain black screen, so avoid doing so!
title: MORDHAU

notes: Ran perfectly on my Arch System with xfce4 on X11 with 2160x3840 res., g-sync worked as well. Don't see much of a difference between this and windows 10 1809. Though i wouldn't recommend using kde plasma and gnome - based desk. env. with NVIDIA gpu's. They seem to get harsh stutters and are unstable(both on Xorg and Wayland). Something more simple like xfce4 works without any problems and unlikely to cause problems of their own.
title: MORDHAU

title: MORDHAU

notes: Out of the box, on ultra settings, game have major frame rate drops when it gets crowdy or there are a lot of effects (like fire). The Grad map in Invasion mode is a good example/test for that. After disabling Esync game works in 50-60 FPS for most of the time. After decreasing shadows, effects and AA I was able to get more stable FPS. There are some rare FPS drops but it happens as often as on Windows 10. Game is playable but does not feel as smooth as on Windows.
title: MORDHAU
